label: Wheat Rust (Disease)
description: Wheat leaves showing reddish-brown pustules or orange dust-like spores indicating leaf rust.
action: Rust detected. Apply fungicides like tebuconazole and plant resistant varieties.

label: Healthy Wheat
description: Green, vibrant healthy wheat crop with no signs of discoloration or spots.
action: Crop looks healthy. Continue standard irrigation and fertilization.

label: Rice Blast (Disease)
description: Rice leaves with diamond-shaped lesions, gray centers, and brown borders.
action: Blast symptoms. Improve water management and avoid excessive nitrogen.

label: Healthy Rice
description: Healthy green rice paddy field with no visible lesions.
action: Healthy crop. Maintain water levels.

label: Corn Leaf Blight
description: Corn leaves with long, cigar-shaped grey-green lesions.
action: Blight detected. Rotate crops and use resistant hybrids.

label: Tomato Early Blight
description: Tomato lower leaves with concentric ring spots (bullseye pattern) and yellowing.
action: Early blight. Remove infected leaves and apply copper-based fungicide.

label: Potato Late Blight
description: Potato leaves with dark water-soaked spots and white fungal growth on undersides.
action: Late blight is serious. Apply fungicide immediately and destroy infected haulms.

label: Cotton Bollworm Damage
description: Cotton bolls with visible holes or presence of caterpillar/worm.
action: Bollworm damage. Use pheromone traps and biological control like Trichogramma.

label: General Pest Damage (Leaves)
description: Leaves of crop showing chewed holes, scalloped edges, or skeletonization.
action: Pest activity visible. Inspect for insects and apply neem oil.

label: Nutrient Deficiency (Nitrogen)
description: Plant leaves turning yellow overall, starting from older leaves (chlorosis).
action: Nitrogen deficiency likely. Apply urea or compost.

label: Dry Soil / Drought
description: Cracked dry soil or wilting crop plants due to lack of water.
action: Water stress detected. Irrigate immediately.

label: Chilli Leaf Curl
description: Curled, thickened and deformed leaves of chilli plant, often looking like a boat shape or upward/downward curl.
action: Leaf curl detected. Use Neem oil, Fipronil or Acephate to control Thrips and Mites.

label: Groundnut Tikka Leaf Spot
description: Dark brown or black circular spots on groundnut leaves, often surrounded by yellow halos.
action: Tikka disease detected. Apply Carbendazim or Mancozeb fungicide and ensure crop rotation.

label: Rice Bacterial Leaf Blight
description: Paddy leaves with yellow to white wavy stripes or streaks along the leaf margins, appearing partially dried.
action: Bacterial Blight. Reduce nitrogen use, drain excess water, and spray Streptocycline with Copper Oxychloride.

label: Healthy Chilli
description: Strong upright chilli plant with vibrant green leaves, blooming white flowers or glossy green/red fruits.
action: Chilli plant looks healthy. Maintain regular soil moisture and organic mulching.

label: Sugarcane Red Rot
description: Sugarcane stems or leaves showing internal red discoloration with white cross-wise patches when cut.
action: Serious Red Rot infection. Remove infected clumps immediately and use healthy seed material for next season.

label: Rice Stem Borer (Dead Heart)
description: Central leaf whorl of paddy dried up and can be easily pulled out; base of the shoot may show borer exit holes.
action: Stem Borer detected. Use pheromone traps (5/acre) or apply Cartap Hydrochloride granules in soil.

label: Cotton Pink Bollworm (Rosette Flower)
description: Cotton flowers that fail to open properly and look like a rosette shape; bolls showing internal staining or entry holes.
action: Pink Bollworm attack. Install pheromone traps and spray Profenofos or Emamectin Benzoate if threshold is reached.

label: Maize Fall Armyworm
description: Large ragged holes in maize leaves, presence of dark green frass (excreta) in the whorl, and striped caterpillars.
action: Fall Armyworm detected. Apply Neem cake in whorls or spray Spinetoram/Chlorantraniliprole for control.

label: Rice Sheath Blight
description: Large greenish-grey lesions on rice leaf sheaths with purple-brown borders, resembling snakeskin.
action: Sheath Blight detected. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilizer and spray Hexaconazole or Validamycin.

label: Banana Bunchy Top
description: Stunted banana plant with short, narrow, crowded leaves at the top giving a 'bunched' look with yellow margins.
action: Bunchy Top Virus. Rogue out (destroy) infected plants immediately to prevent spread and control aphids.

label: Chilli Fruit Rot (Anthracnose)
description: Sunken, circular or necrotic spots on chilli fruits with black dot-like structures (acervuli).
action: Fruit Rot. Spray Carbendazim or Mancozeb and avoid overhead irrigation during fruiting.

label: Healthy Maize
description: Broad, green leaves of maize plant with no tearing or frass; sturdy stem and healthy developing cobs.
action: Maize crop is healthy. Ensure balanced NPK application and monitor for FAW scouting.

label: Healthy Cotton
description: Green, heart-shaped cotton leaves and healthy white/pink flowers with no signs of sucking pest damage.
action: Cotton crop looks healthy. Maintain proper drainage and continue scouting for sucking pests.

label: Healthy Sugarcane
description: Tall Sugarcane plants with vibrant green leaves and strong, uniform stalks with no internal discoloration.
action: Sugarcane is healthy. Ensure timely earthing-up and nitrogen top-dressing.
